development.rule: Developed on the deterministic non-sample C0 dev pool before any sample-unit label. Exactly one primary category per census unit; secondary tags never enter primary aggregation.

categories.definition: Externally observable behavior, refusal, default, or user-facing promise not better described by a more specific category.
What a component is responsible for or explicitly does not own; includes scope and handoff boundaries but not tree placement itself.
Parent/child structure, routing, file or capability ownership, and which component sits where in the module graph.

A frozen fact or conclusion learned from an experiment, benchmark, measurement, comparison, or systematic observation. The learned result and its scope are the content; this is not an outward contract, a reason for choosing a design, an execution procedure, or a narrative of a past incident.
The reason, tradeoff, or rejected alternative that explains why a contract has its current shape.
A state, quantity, ordering, identity, or safety property that must hold across transitions.
How an operation proceeds or is triggered when the mechanism itself carries information beyond the outward behavior.
A past failure, regression, migration hazard, or learned incident whose value depends on historical memory; always secondary even if a cell is numerically sufficient.

agreementGate.metric: Cohen kappa on the two raw primary-category labels
agreementGate.failure: NO-GO after one taxonomy revision and complete blind relabel
